title: Carya pallida
taxonomy: Carya pallida is classified as a species of tree in the family Juglandaceae, commonly known as the walnut family. It belongs to the genus Carya, which is a group of deciduous trees native to North America. The genus Carya is further divided into several species, with Carya pallida being one of the native species found in the United States. Carya pallida is a member of the order Fagales, which includes other trees such as beeches and hazelnuts. This classification helps to identify and distinguish Carya pallida from other tree species.

intro: Carya pallida, also known as the pale shagbark hickory, is a deciduous tree native to the eastern United States and southeastern Canada. The pale shagbark hickory has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and research has shown that it may have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
